titleStmt.title: Letter from Ernest Blythe to George Gavan Duffy, 18 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Ernest Blythe

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Earnest Blythe (1889-1975) to George Gavan Duffy (1882-1951). Blythe requests Gavan Duffy to represent him in court as he is to be prosecuted for failure to report to police in Abingdon, England. Included in the letter is a transcript of the deportation order which was sent by Major-General L.B. Friend to Blythe, 7 April 1916. George Gavan Duffy was an Irish politician, barrister and judge. He unsuccessfully defended Roger Casement at his trial for high treason after the Easter Rising. Ernest Blythe was an organiser for the Irish Volunteers. He had been arrested under the Defence of the Realm Act. He was released from prison in England before the Easter Rising but was rearrested there during Easter week 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Bureau of Military History Contemporary Documents, George Gavan Duffy Collection, BMH CD/45/3/11/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
In replying to this letter, please write on the envelope :- Number 635,Name E. Blythe Oxford Prison 18 April 1916 Dear Sir, I should be
obliged if you would
act for me at Abingdon
on Thursday next when
I shall be prosecuted
for failing to report to
the police as directed
in the enclosed order
I shall be brought before the court at 12 midday Yours sincerely
Ernest Blythe
Copy of Order Headquarters Irish Command Parkgate Dublin 7th April 1916 Ernest Blythe, You have been informed that the
competent military authorities in England have
approved of Abingdon in Oxfordshire as your new
place of residence. You are provided with a single ticket to
Crewe & 13/- railway fare to Abingdon and you
will proceed forth with to Abingdon on arrival
there you will notify your arrival to the local
police and your new place of residence to the competent
Military Authority through the local police.
You are warned that in the event of your
returning to Ireland you will be arrested &
tried in England E.B. Friend Major General
Competent Military etc.
Authority.
